entry.title: Re: 2608 C4C Release Content
entry.content:
The 2608 pre-release information for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud hasn’t been published yet. As soon as this information is available, you will be able to find it on "What’s New "notes and release highlights shortly before general availability. Please keep an eye on:

  • SAP Release Navigator 
  • SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps (Customer Experience)*

We will continue tracking the relevant 2608 release information in these portals. As soon as the materials and webinars are available, we will publish and share the details there.

*To access Value Map Groups, you must log in to SAP Community with an S-user ID or a Universal ID.

entry.title: Using Agentic AI as an Intelligent Decision Layer for SAP PM
entry.content:
A Conceptual Approach to Smarter Condition-Based Maintenance


Introduction
As manufacturing plants continue to adopt IoT and condition monitoring, equipment generates an ever-increasing amount of sensor data. Parameters such as vibration, temperature, pressure and flow provide valuable insights into equipment health.
The real challenge, however, is not collecting the data—it's deciding which sensor events actually require maintenance action.


Many organizations still rely on fixed thresholds or manual analysis by maintenance planners before creating SAP PM notifications. This article proposes a conceptual architecture where an AI Agent acts as an intelligent decision layer, ensuring that only meaningful events result in maintenance notifications.

Rather than replacing existing SAP PM processes, this architecture aims to improve the quality of maintenance notifications by introducing intelligence before notification creation.

1. From Equipment to SAP – The Current Approach

The journey begins with equipment continuously transmitting health information through multiple sensors. These sensor readings are transferred into SAP through an IoT platform or middleware.

While this provides excellent visibility into equipment health, every sensor event does not necessarily indicate a maintenance issue. Temporary operating conditions or minor fluctuations can generate alerts that require manual review, increasing the workload for maintenance planners.
The challenge is no longer collecting data—it's identifying which data actually deserves attention.


2. Proposed Architecture – Introducing an AI Decision Layer

The AI agent acts only as a decision layer. Once a notification is created, the standard SAP PM notification-to-work order process continues unchanged.

Instead of creating a notification for every sensor event, an AI Agent evaluates the incoming information before SAP PM is involved.
The AI analyzes equipment behaviour using historical trends, operational context and multiple sensor readings to determine whether the detected event represents:
      Normal operating behaviour
     Early equipment degradation
     A genuine maintenance issue


Only events classified as actionable result in an SAP PM maintenance notification.
Once the notification is created, the standard SAP PM process continues unchanged, including planning, work order creation, scheduling and execution.


Finally, technician feedback can be used to continuously improve future AI decisions, allowing the system to become more accurate over time.


The objective is not to replace SAP PM or maintenance planners, but to improve the quality of information entering the maintenance process.


3. Potential Benefits

Introducing an AI decision layer before notification creation could provide several operational benefits:

Reduced planner effort by filtering unnecessary sensor events before they become notifications.

Improved notification quality through richer context and better identification of genuine maintenance issues. 

Earlier detection of equipment degradation, enabling proactive rather than reactive maintenance.

Better equipment availability by reducing unnecessary downtime and focusing maintenance activities where they add the most value.

Continuous improvement, as technician feedback helps refine future AI decisions.

The exact benefits will naturally depend on each organization's equipment, processes and implementation approach.


Technical Considerations
Although this article focuses on the concept, several implementation questions remain open for discussion:

Should the AI run within SAP BTP or as an external service?

How should AI confidence influence notification priority?

What should happen when the AI cannot confidently classify an event?

How should technician feedback be incorporated into the learning process?

Conclusion

Condition monitoring has made equipment data more accessible than ever before. The next challenge is transforming that data into meaningful maintenance decisions.

Adding an AI decision layer before SAP PM notification creation offers one possible approach to reducing unnecessary notifications while improving maintenance efficiency. Rather than changing the existing SAP PM process, it enhances the quality of information entering it.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud – release update highlights
entry.content: is Business Data Cloud zero copy delta share connector to Snowflake available on Azure or just AWS ?

entry.title: Re: What is the recommended approach for vendor-integrati...
entry.content: Any update on this process and exact steps needed to integrate with vendors (particularly those already in the Marketplace)?

entry.title: Re: FAQ: Utilities Sales Process in SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Thank you for the excellent session. Also very curios about how to fulfill utility requirements bundling commodity and non-commodity products and services. Here in North America, we mostly work with regulated utilities.
But utilities of all sizes, even down to smaller Co-Ops have the requirement to sell bundles of commodity&non-commodity products and services.
In this case, would we then recommend using the simple and complex sales process to cover commodity&non-commodity sales?
Or would we rather recommend the traditional Move-In/Out for commodity sales and complex sales for commodity&non-commodity?

Maybe this is already a question for the next Espresso session about the complex sales process! 🙂

entry.title: Re: How to perform bulk External ID Mapping for Business Partners in SAP Service Cloud V2
entry.content: Hi, can you please advise if you were able to successfully update the ID mapping in bulk? If so, can you please share the details. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: RTS - Return to supplier | RTV - Return to vendor | A...
entry.content: Hello @manishguptaofficial  - This is very common issue with so many possible resolutions, you need to check the Configuration for "Shipping Data for Plants" in STO Configuration check the material is extended to the sales area or not, loading group is maintained in Material master record and plant is assigned to shipping point etc.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business AI Platform partner-led adoption incentive

Oops, the page you requested cannot be accessed.

 

We apologize for the inconvenience, but the content you're trying to access is not available to everyone.

 

Consider expanding your engagement with SAP to gain full access to exclusive content, support, and resources.

Learn more about the different partnership options and how they can benefit your business.


Requested Page: https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/jun/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014395.html

entry.title: Re: Automatic sales order creation
entry.content:
Obrigado pela sua pergunta, @35400__ .
No SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, o processo padrão para contratos de serviços funciona de forma um pouco diferente do que criar automaticamente pedidos de venda de forma recorrente.

✅ Resposta curta

  • A criação automática de pedidos de venda a partir de um contrato de serviço não faz parte do padrão.
  • Em vez disso, os contratos de serviço usam planos de faturamento periódicos, que geram as faturas diretamente (sem necessidade de pedidos de venda intermediários).

✅ Abordagem recomendada

1) Utilizar contrato de serviço com plano de faturamento periódico

  • Configure os itens do contrato com um plano de faturamento periódico (por exemplo, mensal).
  • O sistema cria automaticamente linhas de faturamento para cada data.
  • Essas linhas representam as futuras faturas.
✔ Em resumo:
O sistema gera automaticamente as datas e valores de faturamento com base no plano.

2) Gerar automaticamente as faturas

  • Agende o job “Schedule Billing Document Request Creation”.
  • Esse job transforma as linhas do plano em solicitações de faturamento (BDRs).
  • A partir dessas, você gera as faturas (manualmente ou automaticamente).

3) Esclarecimento importante sobre pedidos de venda

  • Pedidos de venda:
    • São normalmente criados manualmente ou via integração
  • No contexto de contratos:
    • Eles são usados como ordens de liberação (call-off)
    • Não são gerados automaticamente de forma recorrente

✅ Quando usar pedidos de venda?

Use pedidos de venda somente se:
  • Houver necessidade de logística/entrega, ou
  • Você precisar liberar quantidades/valores contra o contrato
Para faturamento recorrente puro, eles não são necessários.

✅ Alternativa (cenário mais avançado)

Se o seu cenário for mais parecido com subscription (uso recorrente, consumo etc.), vale avaliar:
  • SAP Subscription Billing

✅ Resumo

  • ✔ Use contrato de serviço + plano de faturamento periódico
  • ✔ Agende o job de geração de faturamento
  • ❌ Não espere criação automática de pedidos de venda
  • ✔ As faturas são geradas diretamente a partir do contrato
Se puder compartilhar mais detalhes (tipo de contrato ou necessidade de logística), posso ajudar a refinar ainda mais a solução 🙂
Nota de transparência de IA:
Esta resposta foi preparada com o suporte de IA e validada com base na documentação do SAP Help Portal. Por favor, verifique sempre de acordo com a configuração e a versão do seu sistema.
 
Se esta resposta atendeu à sua necessidade, por favor marque-a como tal. Obrigado.

......

For transparency, translated to American English with a German accent, SAP's lingua franca ;-):
 

1) Question


Hello,
I would like to understand the process to automatically create a sales order from a service contract. I have tried, but I wasn’t successful.
What would be the best approach to automatically generate the sales order and the monthly invoice based on a service contract?
Scenario: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

✅ 2) Answer

Thanks for your question.
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the standard process for service contracts works a bit differently from automatically creating sales orders on a recurring basis.

✅ Short answer

  • Automatic creation of sales orders from a service contract is not the standard approach.
  • Instead, service contracts use periodic billing plans to directly generate invoices (via billing document requests)—without creating sales orders in between.

✅ Recommended approach

1) Use a Service Contract with a Periodic Billing Plan

  • Maintain your service contract items with a periodic billing plan (e.g., monthly billing).
  • The system automatically generates billing request lines for each billing date.
    👉 These billing request lines represent your future invoices.
  • This is the standard mechanism for recurring service billing.
  • Reference: Periodic Billing Plans for Service Contract Billing
✔ Key behavior:
  • “The system generates one billing request line for each billing date in the billing plan.” [help.sap.com]

2) Automatically Generate Billing Documents (Invoices)

  • Schedule the job “Schedule Billing Document Request Creation”.
  • This job converts billing plan lines into billing document requests (BDRs).
  • Then invoices can be created from these requests (manually or via automation).
  • Reference: Creating and Processing Billing Documents
✔ Key behavior:
  • Billing for service contracts relies on BDRs generated from billing plans [help.sap.com]

3) Important clarification about Sales Orders

  • Sales orders are typically:
    • Created manually, or
    • Created via APIs / integrations / specific scenarios
  • For contracts, sales orders are usually release orders (call-offs) and are not automatically generated by the system on a schedule.
  • Reference: Sales Contract Fulfillment
✔ Key behavior:
  • Sales contracts are fulfilled through sales orders created as releases [help.sap.com]

✅ When would you need Sales Orders?

You only need sales orders if:
  • There is a logistics or fulfillment process, or
  • You explicitly want to create “release orders” against a contract
For recurring service billing scenarios, they are not required.

✅ Optional alternative (if subscription-like behavior is needed)

If your scenario is closer to subscription billing (e.g., usage-based or complex recurring pricing):
  • You can evaluate SAP Subscription Billing integration
  • Reference: Subscription Management with Sales Billing

✅ Summary

  • ✔ Use service contracts + periodic billing plans
  • ✔ Schedule billing document request creation jobs
  • ❌ Do not expect automatic sales order creation as part of standard recurring billing
  • ✔ Invoices are generated directly from the contract billing plan

✅ Follow‑up (to refine guidance)

To confirm the best recommendation, could you clarify:
  • Are you working with service contracts or sales contracts, and
  • Do you require logistics execution or only recurring billing?

✅ AI transparency note

This response was prepared with AI‑assisted support and validated against SAP Help Portal documentation. Please verify against your system configuration and release.
 
If this response addresses your request to your satisfaction, please mark it accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: how to create project billing request using API in sa...

Dear @Anushka__Khanduri

SAP Business Accelerator Hub
I'm using this API that you shared in the comment.

In the Project Billing Request API function POST to CreateProjectBillingRequest



The BillingWBSElementInternalID is required field to post, but I want to know which data need to put BillingWBSElementInternalID field when using Post Function.
I attached in this post, billing element data in the "Manage project billing" is more than 8 string.

Kindly check once again?
Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Automatic sales order creation
entry.content: @AndreasMuno Thank you for the response! That makes a lot of sense. I was trying to fit this into the sales order model, but a periodic billing plan directly on the contract solves exactly what I needed. A couple of follow-up questions to close out my understanding: Is this flow supported for Brazil? The contract types available in Manage Sales Contracts are quantity and value contracts. I did some research and it looks like I'd need to activate a service contract type — how would I go about activating that? Can the periodic billing plan be set up directly on a service contract, or does it require a separate configuration step to enable it at that document type? Thanks again for the help!

entry.title: Re: Automatic sales order creation
entry.content: @AndreasMuno Thank you for the response! Is this flow supported for Brazil? The contract types available in Manage Sales Contracts are quantity and value contracts. I did some research and it looks like I'd need to activate a service contract type — how would I go about activating that? Can the periodic billing plan be set up directly on a service contract, or does it require a separate configuration step to enable it at that document type? Thanks again for the help!

entry.title: Re: CWE 377 Insecure Temporary File vulnerability in Crys...
entry.content:
Crystal Reports 11.5 is end of Life now, no more patches available.

And the CR SDK's are no longer shipped with Crystal Reports.

You can upgrade to CR for Eclipse now:

https://www.eclipse.org/downloads/

It will require you to update your project and references.

entry.title: Re: Crlf injection
entry.content:
See your other post:

CWE 377 Insecure Temporary File vulnerability in Crystal Reports Java Runtime 11.8.6.1371

entry.title: Public Cloud material master data migration, template missing Costing 1 - special procurement type
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I am doing material master data migration in Public Cloud. Because we want to use field: Costing 1 - special procurement type, but when I wanted to upload this field found that there is no this field in the standard material master uploading template, but only in MRP and Procurement tab. What's the logic behind, and how to proceed if we still want to use and upload field: Costing 1 - special procurement type. 

If we can NOT do that, will Uploading without this field + Mass change material work for this?

 

 

 

Thanks a lot!

Alice

entry.title: Re: Applying prerequisite SAP Notes separately to Product...
entry.content: Hi RaymondGiuseppi, thanks for your reply. It's really helpful.

entry.title: Recommended Approach to Change Fiscal Year Variant for an Existing Set of Books in SAP ByD
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to seek advice regarding the possibility of changing the fiscal year variant for an existing set of books in SAP Business ByDesign.

Currently, the set of books is configured with fiscal year variant 39 - Nov FY-1 to Oct, meaning that the fiscal year runs from November to October. We would like to change the fiscal year variant so that the fiscal year runs from May to April.

Journal entries and other accounting transactions have already been posted under the current fiscal year variant. We would therefore like to understand whether there is any SAP-recommended best-practice approach or alternative solution to change the fiscal year variant for the existing set of books.

We have also referred to KBA 2013755 - Fiscal Year Variant Cannot be Changed, which states: 

  • This change can be performed just for a new fiscal year, derived from a new set of books which would be using the desired Fiscal Year Variant.

Based on the above statement, we would appreciate any advice or experience regarding the following questions:

  1. Does this mean that we are required to create a new set of books with the desired fiscal year variant?
  2. If a new set of books is required, how should the financial data be transitioned from the existing set of books to the new set of books? Would this require a new migration of accounting data, including G/L balances, open AR/AP items, fixed assets, and other relevant balances?
  3. Other than creating a new set of books, is there any other SAP-recommended approach or workaround for changing the fiscal year variant when accounting transactions have already been posted?

We would greatly appreciate any guidance or experience from the community regarding the recommended approach for this scenario.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
HY

entry.title: Re: From SAP Integration Suite iFlow to MCP Tools: Exposing OData APIs to AI Agents
entry.content: @Ananta_Paul79 currently MCP server is available in enhanced and premium editions only.

entry.title: Re: Unable to release Network activity in CJ20N
entry.content:
Refer to SAP Note 678522 - FAQ 2: Data inconsistencies in the Project System

Look at Section 3 and 4 for possible fixes.

entry.title: Clean Core Levels A–D: how to classify your custom ABAP — and what to do with each grade
entry.content:
Disclosure: I'm the author of this post and the creator of Clean-Core.io, a free, non-commercial community tool I mention once near the end. This post is educational; the tool is optional.

Overview

With the 2025 update to the ABAP extensibility guide, SAP evolved the familiar 3-tier extensibility model into the clean core level concept — a four-grade classification of custom extensions: A, B, C, D. Instead of a blunt "clean vs. not clean", it tells you how closely each extension aligns with Clean Core, and it maps directly to ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) behaviour:

 

Level Usage of SAP objects in custom development ATC behaviour

AReleased SAP APIs (local & remote) and extension pointsNo message
BClassic SAP APIs and extension points (SAP-recommended)Priority 3 · info
CInternal SAP APIs (conditionally clean via the changelog-for-SAP-objects check)Priority 2 · warning
DNot-recommended objects & extension technologies (modifications, implicit enhancements)Priority 1 · error

Level A is the cleanest — on-stack ABAP Cloud or side-by-side on SAP BTP. Levels B–D describe classic ABAP, from SAP-recommended (B) down to not-recommended (D).

Why this matters now

Every custom object you carry into S/4HANA has to be defensible. The move from a binary "clean/not clean" to A–D is a gift — it lets you transform at your own pace, prioritising the worst first instead of trying to reach "100% clean" overnight.

The real problem

Knowing the model is easy. Grading thousands of custom objects against it is not. ATC flags readiness object-by-object, and the Cloudification Repository tells you which standard objects are released — but turning that into a per-object A/B/C/D verdict plus a remediation path, across a grown Z-landscape, is still mostly manual and inconsistent between teams.

A practical classification flow

  1. Identify the custom object and what it touches (tables, APIs, function modules, enhancements).
  2. Look up each dependency: released API/extension point → A; classic-but-official API → B; internal SAP object → C; modification / not-recommended access → D.
  3. Decide remediation per grade: A keep · B keep, plan to lift later · C verify via the changelog-for-SAP-objects approach and wrap behind a released interface · D replace before the upgrade.

A worked example

A classic Z_CUSTOMER_REPORT:

SELECT kunnr, name1 FROM kna1 INTO TABLE @lt_cust.   " direct read of an internal standard table

Classify: direct access to KNA1 (an internal SAP object, not a released API) → Level C (ATC: warning). A direct write would be Level D (ATC: error).

Remediate: map to the released CDS view I_Customer → the same logic becomes Level A (ATC: no message):

SELECT Customer, CustomerName FROM I_Customer INTO TABLE @lt_cust.

One dependency, one grade, one clear next step. Repeat across the landscape and you get a prioritised backlog: fix D first (upgrade blockers), lift C, schedule B, keep A.

How I approached tooling this (optional)

To avoid doing this by hand, I built Clean-Core.io — a free, non-commercial community tool. It runs a deterministic evidence engine first (parsing the code, mapping dependencies against the catalog), then AI on top, and derives an A–D grade per detected object — aligned to the clean core level concept and its ATC priorities. It is complementary to ATC/ADT, not a replacement, and every output is a draft for a qualified architect to review. Free: clean-core.io (link in the comments).

Conclusion

The move from 3-tier to A–D isn't a relabel — it's a better mental model for prioritising custom-code remediation. Give each dependency an explicit grade, start with your Level D objects, and turn "we have a lot of Z-code" into a defensible, upgrade-ready plan.

How are you classifying your custom code against the new A–D levels today — by hand, ATC, or something else? I'd genuinely like to compare approaches in the comments.

entry.title: How to validate manually entered batch numbers during batch creation or saving in SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
I am looking for a way to validate manually entered batch numbers during the batch creation or saving process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
Could you please advise if there are any available enhancements, BAdIs, or extensibility options (such as Custom Logic) to achieve this validation?
Any insights, configuration steps, or relevant documentation links would be highly appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | Restriction Field:...
entry.content: Thank you for your answer.

entry.title: Re: Copying the files to my source tree
entry.content:
Provided that you adhere to the underlying SQL Anywhere license, the three (3) files in SDK\dbcapi  are licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0.  You can add those files to your source control system provided the comment block containing the copyright and the Apache License verbiage remain unmodified. You are permitted to modify other aspects of the source files as discussed in the final paragraph of the comment block as there is no restriction in the Apache license for such changes.

If those files are from the Developer Edition, please review the license term 3 b  which only permits the copy and deployment  internally up to twenty-five (25) copies for testing and prototyping.  This might limit the checkouts from the source control system  to that number (or possibly one less if the source control is considered a copy).  Applications making use of these files are not permitted to deploy or use any of the client components i.e., dbcapi and its dependencies, whether using static or dynamic library, for production use without a suitable "production" license.  Those client binaries are not covered by the Apache Licensing.

entry.title: Re: CWE 377 Insecure Temporary File vulnerability in Crys...

entry.title: Re: Introducing the SAP Service and Asset Manager Setup Wizard
entry.content:
Hi @keithblake ,

Thank you for sharing this informative blog. It will definitely save a lot of time . I appreciate the effort you put into documenting the process.

Does the solution include any features to support system copy activities, such as preserving selected tables and restoring them after the refresh? I'm particularly interested in preserving SSAM-related configuration.

entry.title: Re: How to post Free of Charge Delivery (BDA) inventory c...
entry.content: Hi Lakshmipathi, Thank you very much for your detailed explanation and for taking the time to answer my question. I really appreciate your help. I have one follow-up question regarding the proposed Split Valuation approach. In our scenario, the same material can be either sold through a standard sales order or delivered free of charge as a sample (CBFD). Our concern is that activating Split Valuation is a strategic inventory design decision and would require maintaining separate valuation types and inventory processes for the same material. From your experience: Would you consider Split Valuation as SAP's recommended best practice specifically for this business requirement, or is it simply one possible technical solution? Additionally, have you ever implemented this requirement using the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules (Journal Entry) app or another Clean Core extensibility approach instead of changing the inventory valuation model? Thank you again for your insights.

entry.title: Data Load Issue in Cost Center Due to Invalid Characters
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are unable to load data into the Cost Center from CSKS table due to an issue related to invalid characters.

We have already tried allowing some special characters in RSKC, but the issue still persists.

Could you please provide your suggestions or guidance on how to resolve this issue?

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Re: Asset not fully depreciating with Dep to day approach...
entry.content:
Dear Team.,

when you have depreciation to the day you need to add in the depreciation key change over method in the first phase of the depreciation key e.g. change over method 5 and in the second phase you add a base method e.g. 0017 immediately depreciation after the end of useful life.

BR

Werner

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAC Story Buttons Not Working in Presentation Mode Wh...
entry.content: One possible reason is lite view mode is triggered when directly open story via URL in a fresh browser tab, and somehow the JS logic behind the button is working properly. I would recommend to create a ticket to SAP on this.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content:
Hi SAP Learning Hub SMEs

I am trying to purchase a Learning Hub subscription as an individual, and it's proving to be extremely difficult.  Why doesn't SAP allow this to be smoother? Why does the individual trying to purchase need to add company details? I have raised a ticket to have this solved, and 2 weeks on - it's still not resolved. I guess I am just venting, as I can't understand why SAP is making it so hard for someone to purchase an LH subscription - not for free.

entry.title: Signavio integration with SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have tried to add Signavio attributes to the producer workspace. but I dont see the attributes in the dropdown. 

Please help me to integrate signavio with Enable now.

Sharing the User details and error details screenshots in attachment

Thanks
Nagaraj

entry.title: Re: Hide Tabs on IQ03
entry.content:
Yes,

this is technically feasible, but it cannot be achieved through standard SAP configuration. IQ03 does not provide a standard option to hide tabs dynamically based on the Sales Organization.

The requirement would need to be analyzed for available enhancement points (BAdI/User Exit). If no suitable enhancement exists, it can be implemented through an implicit enhancement or screen modification in the IQ03 dynpro.

entry.title: Re: deletion task /operation in pm order
entry.content:
This is not possible through standard SAP configuration. During planned maintenance order creation, the system copies all operations from the assigned task list according to the standard logic.

Alternate Solution

1. If you need to remove a specific operation dynamically, you'll need to implement a BAdI/User Exit or another enhancement during order creation.

2. Alternatively, if the operation should never be included for certain scenarios, maintaining separate task lists or maintenance strategies is the recommended standard approach.

entry.title: Re: AADSTS90072 when accessing newly assigned SAP Practic...
entry.content:
Issues I am facing:

  1. No Fiori Spaces or Pages are visible on my launchpad (it's completely empty).

  2. The Joule (AI assistant) icon is missing.

  3. I need access to the Manage Business Users app.

entry.title: 创建销项发票界面coding block扩展字段配置
entry.content: 在“自定义字段”应用中如何配置相关属性可以在“创建销项发票”应用界面中展示该自定义字段，包括业务上下文选择及需要启用的用户界面、CDS视图等

entry.title: Re: Signavio integration with SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi Nagaraj,

At the 14min mark of this video, you can see the steps that are necessary to add the two custom attributes to the Signavio Workspace.  They are added through Producer but are added to the Signavio Workspace.

If you are seeing something different when you try to use the capability in Producer to add the attributes, please add a screenshot of what you see or raise a support ticket for Signavio as I suspect that you may not have the relevant permissions.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Nice article.

entry.title: Re: How to post Free of Charge Delivery (BDA) inventory c...
entry.content:
• Split Valuation is not SAP’s recommended best practice for free‑of‑charge deliveries; it’s a structural option if your business already needs valuation differentiation.

  • For your requirement, SAP generally recommends using substitution/validation rules in Finance or other clean‑core extensibility to redirect postings, rather than redesigning inventory valuation.

This way, you preserve standard logistics behavior (movement type 601, CBFD/CBXN scope item) but achieve the desired accounting outcome in Finance.

entry.title: Re: Required MBEW - Moving price Time stamp
entry.content: Screen shot is missing

entry.title: Transferring newly created material to SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm looking for a functionality of transferring newly created material to SAP DM, all configurations and integrations are in place. System is able to trigger MATMAS Idoc when sending from BD10.

But I have scheduled a job in the background for the program - RBDMIDOC (BD21) and after creating a new material via MM01, system is sending all the materials to SAP DM. How to set criteria or filter only to send newly created material.

And in BDCP2 table there are some logs available not processed yet, but its a mix of multiple message types and for MATMAS only 64 entries are showing.

 

 

Regards,

Rahaman

entry.title: Re: Teaching SAP Without Teaching SAP
entry.content: This is great information to share. However, I’m not an academic, but I used to be a student. I really understand your point of view, that students must first understand basic business processes before learning an ERP system like SAP. But once you understand your basic business processes, you’ll be able to use not only SAP but also all other ERP software.

entry.title: Re: Understanding Valuated and Non-Valuated Project Stock in SAP: What Really Happens During GR and
entry.content: thank you for sharing

entry.title: Updated Stay Certified Programs for TM and EWM
entry.content:
We are glad to announce the updates of our Stay Certified programs for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editon, Extended Warehouse Management and Transportation Management. You can find the new assets (recorded live sessions) via the following links:

  • Stay Certified for EWM 
  • Stay Certified for TM 

What is Stay Certified?

Stay Certified is SAP's program for maintaining up-to-date certifications by completing periodic update learning modules and assessments designed for specific roles.

Which certifications are associated with this update?

  • The Stay Certified program for EWM is associated with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Extended Warehouse Management certification (C_S4EWM).
  • The Stay Certified program for TM is associated with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Transportation Management certification (C_S4TM).

How do I take the Stay Certified assessment?

To take the Stay Certified assessment, follow these steps:

  1. Log in to the learning.sap.com account.
  2. Click on "My Certifications" under your profile. You will then see a list of all your certifications.
  3.  Click on "Manage certification". 

 

  1. Click on the "Take Assessment" button.

Note: Access to the Stay Certified programs requires a subscription on the learning.sap.com platform.

entry.title: A New-Era of Learning for Support from SAP - Support Accreditation 2026 Release is coming soon!
entry.content:
Support Accreditation has long been the foundation for enabling customers, partners, and employees to successfully engage with support from SAP.

With its 2026 release, the program undergoes its most significant evolution to date - redesigned to reflect modern learning approaches, the latest advancements in SAP’s support services, and extensive feedback from customers and learners.

Built for a new era of AI-driven, proactive, and personalized support, the updated experience focuses on one clear goal: enabling users to resolve issues faster, make better decisions, and maximize the value of SAP. 

Stay tuned for more updates coming your way in the next 10 days!

entry.title: 销售订单后续后勤活动控制
entry.content:
业务场景

1. 当创建销售订单触发审批流时，如果销售单未获批准或正处于审批流程中，则无需触发库存需求。在APP管理物料覆盖范围（及MRP有关APP）中，必须阻止或不显示该销售订单物料需求的转移。

2. 关于第三方销售订单场景，当销售订单创建且审批状态为“待审批”时，系统将生成该销售订单的采购需求。然而，若销售订单信息由多个部门共同审批，则内容可能存在修改。此时，销售订单中的交货日期、数量及其他采购相关信息需要进行调整。如果销售订单的审批状态为“待审批”时生成采购需求，此时采购部门将根据该需求向供应商发出采购订单。在审批完成后，若销售订单内容中对交货日期和数量进行了修改，则基于“待审批”需求发出的采购订单将与实际需求不符，严重影响企业的正常工作。需求控制第三方销售订单处于审批时，不生成采购申请。

3. 当销售订单被信用冻结后，要求物料需求不会被转至采购或生产环节。

无需手动操作即可将计划行类别更改为CN（无MRP）。当选择确认时的交货限制后，需求转移将被自动阻止。防止在受限订单中分配可用产品，并在确认限制期间阻止进一步的MRP（采购）处理。

系统实现

案例：Make-to-Order Processing

创建销售订单

点击行项目

字段Supply Block Reason设置Item not ready 01

此时订单总体供应链状态会是冻结

查看APP：库存物料清单MD04，发现销售订单11274物料需求没有进入

回到销售订单更改字段Supply Block Status到Not Blocked。

回到APP:库存物料清单MD04，销售订单11274物料需求进入

运行MRP：

生成生产计划订单：

注意：

一旦计划订单创建完成，供应冻结将不再影响MRP。客户可以继续将计划订单转换为生产订单。

建议:

客户可以通过检查未完整检查状态来决定是否设置字段：Supply Block Reason。

案例：第三方销售订单

当销售订单字段Supply Block Reason设置了Item not ready 01，保存后并不会自动生成采购申请

当销售订单更改字段Supply Block Status到Not Blocked，采购申请自动创建。

 

感谢观看

entry.title: Could not load Cloud Foundry organization.
entry.content:
Facing this below issue with my trial account:
Could not load Cloud Foundry organization. Some information on this page may be missing or inaccurate. Try refreshing the page.

entry.title: Re: Error with bank statement import
entry.content:
Hi Dmitrijs,

Short answer: This is an XSD / structure‑validation error raised while the file is parsed against the selected format mapping — not an EBS posting or bank/GL configuration problem. The incoming camt.053 contains a financial‑institution (agent) block that is present but incomplete, or the wrong camt version was selected, so the mandatory identification node can't be read. The fix lives in the file or the format mapping, not in your EBS config.

What the message means
BranchAndFinancialInstitutionIdentification4 is the ISO 20022 complex type that identifies a bank/agent (e.g. DbtrAgt, CdtrAgt, InstgAgt, or the account servicer Svcr) in the camt.053.001.02 generation. Its mandatory child is the financial‑institution identification, FinInstnId. "…must occur at least 1 time" is the parser saying a mandatory occurrence of that block was expected but not found. The token N00551199622 is an internal parser node reference, not a business value — ignore it.

Interpretation: The most likely cause is that one agent element in the file is opened but empty (no FinInstnId inside), or the file's version/namespace doesn't match the format you selected, so the engine can't locate the mandatory node. You have not provided me with more details, so treat the precise failing tag as something to confirm in the file rather than as a fact.

Note: Reproduce the file in Test Incoming Format Mappings (F6960) and read the Log tab — the engine shows the run as "Completed with Errors" and the log points to exactly where the structure/format check failed, without posting anything. App does not have its own tile, it needs to be accessed from Map Format Data apps. Reference: Community Portal

Verify (version match): In Manage Incoming Payment Files (F1680), confirm the chosen format matches the file's xmlns (…camt.053.001.02 vs .06/.08). Mapping/structure errors are raised during import in F1680 and are tied to the specific format‑mapping version selected; a version mismatch produces exactly these "mandatory node missing" errors. Reference: KBA 3159797

Fix path 1 — file side: Ask the bank to deliver a schema‑valid file where every agent element carries a FinInstnId (at least a BIC/BICFI, a clearing‑system member ID, or a name). An empty DbtrAgt / CdtrAgt / Svcr element is invalid per ISO 20022 and will always be rejected.

Fix path 2 — mapping side (if the bank can't change it): For a recurring or localised deviation, copy the standard camt.053 mapping in Map Format Data for Incoming Files from Banks (F5608), adjust the affected agent node so the empty/missing block is tolerated, then re‑validate in F6960 before going back to F1680. This copy‑standard‑then‑adjust‑the‑node pattern is the documented workaround for camt files that deviate from the standard structure. References: KBA 3604846 and Community Portal Article

Constraint: Country‑specific camt formats might not be accurate or available, so if this is a localised file, you build the custom mapping yourself. Reference: KBA 3672538

Summary
Treat this as a structural/file issue: the camt.053 has an empty or missing bank‑identification (FinInstnId) block, or the wrong camt version was picked in F1680. Confirm the version, reproduce in Test Incoming Format Mappings to see the exact failing node, then either get a corrected file from the bank or build a custom mapping in Map Format Data (F5608). Don't change EBS posting configuration for this.

Hope it helps.
Best regards,
Michal

entry.title: Re: SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model What’s New as of 2026-07-06
entry.content: Wonderful, and also the very very useful insights you have shared with us. Thank you alex_marasigan 🙂

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 1 — Model the Board
entry.content:
1. db/schema.cds 
namespace snakeladder;

// ── Status enum — the heart of the state machine ──────────────────────────
type TurnStatus : String enum {
  Waiting;    // Session created, not yet started
  Playing;    // It's this player's turn — roll the dice!
  Moving;     // Dice rolled, player token is being moved
  Blocked;    // Player hit a double-headed snake — skips next turn
  Finished;   // This player has reached square 100 and won
}

// ── Board square — pre-loaded with snake/ladder data ──────────────────────
entity BoardSquares {
  key square      : Integer;        // 1–100
      snakeTo     : Integer;        // 0 if no snake on this square
      ladderTo    : Integer;        // 0 if no ladder on this square
      isDoubleHead: Boolean default false;  // true if this is a double-headed snake mouth
}

// ── Game session ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
entity GameSessions {
  key ID          : UUID;
      name        : String(100);    // e.g. "Friday Afternoon Game"
      createdAt   : Timestamp;
      startedAt   : Timestamp;
      finishedAt  : Timestamp;
      winner      : Association to Players;
}

// ── Player — one row per player per session ───────────────────────────────
@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players {
  key ID          : UUID;
      session     : Association to GameSessions;
      name        : String(100);
      @readonly
      position    : Integer default 1;      // current square (1–100)
      @readonly
      TurnStatus  : TurnStatus default #Waiting;
      turnOrder   : Integer;                // 1 = goes first
      lastRoll    : Integer;               // last dice result
}
 
 srv/game-service.cds

using { snakeladder } from '../db/schema';

service GameService {

  entity GameSessions as projection on snakeladder.GameSessions;

  @flow.status: TurnStatus
  entity Players as projection on snakeladder.Players actions {

    // Week 2 will implement these — declare them now so the model is complete
    @from: [ #Waiting ]
    @TO: #Playing
    action startTurn();

    @from: [ #Playing ]
    @TO: #Moving
    action rollDice() returns Integer;

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Waiting                  // transitions to next player in Week 3
    action confirmMove();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Blocked                  // double-headed snake bite
    action blockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Blocked ]
    @TO: $flow.previous            // restores pre-block status
    action unblockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Finished
    action winGame();
  }

  entity BoardSquares as projection on snakeladder.BoardSquares;
}
 
A screenshot of http://localhost:4004 showing the GameService endpoints

One sentence explaining your choice of TurnStatus enum values — did you add any of your own?

Kept the TurnStatus as it is, as i think its sufficient.

entry.title: Re: New Integration of SAP Cloud Transport Management and SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi @kumareshan_S,

I am facing the same issue. Kindly help to share the steps if you find a solution.

My CPI TRs are visible in cTMS, and I can import them from there. However, when I transport from Development to QA through cTMS, I go to Features and click Assign TRs, but no TRs are displayed.

Additionally, in System Groups, after assigning the systems, when I click the Assign Node option under Systems, no nodes appear in the pop-up window.

entry.title: Withholding Tax calculation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have quite a trouble understanding the logic (or probably we have missed a set up) that caused this computation. 

This is from two items in the billing document, and I am now aware that the SD and FI conditions/computations are not the same. However, I would like to know how the "Taxes on Sales/Purchases Amount" was derived or computed from or is it a configuration set up. The taxes on sales applied to this transaction is 12% but here the amount shows about only 2% (for both items) though they have two different WHT tax types assigned to each item. 

Can someone explain the logic/computation behind this? 

The sample expected computation is: 

Gross amount                       230,777.12
Less Taxes on Sales            - 24,726.12 (12% of 206,051)
Net amount                           206,051.00
Withholding Tax Rate                       20%
Withholding Amount           41,210.20

Also, as I was simulating another scenario trying to understand the computation logic, I created a transaction that had 230,777.12 as the gross amount still, upon simulation, the gross amount shows 1,000 (I have condition record PPR0 set up for 1,000 for this but I changed the amount in the transaction to 230,777.12).

I would like to know how the system derives these amounts. 
Thank you for your patience and help!

entry.content: "Downloading the service catalog", where is it in SAP for ME? Thanks.

Additionally, in System Groups, after assigning the systems, when I click the Assign Node option under Systems, no nodes appear in the pop-up window.

Kindly let me know any further steps to be followed.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - CI - CITS Extension - Generate Technical Specifications Directly from the iFlow Design

entry.title: Customize Your Emails for Situation Handling in the Extended Framework
entry.content:
Situation Handling

Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud alerts users about issues requiring their attention. You can use SAP-delivered templates or create your own use cases to detect critical business issues.

With the extended framework for Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608, you can customize the emails for object-based situations. This blog post explains the default notification settings and the customizing option.

Alert the right users about situations

Situation Handling uses multiple channels to inform the users about critical business situations that require their attention. This includes:

  • Situation descriptions embedded in business apps
  • Notification channels in SAP Fiori launchpad and emails

Situation Handling is designed to keep users well informed and support them in their daily work.

Situation Handling emails

One way of informing users about critical business situations is by email. Emails are triggered together with notifications and sent automatically when situations occur, provided the notification option is activated.

Activate emails together with notifications

Let’s look at the standard notification settings of a situation type and their impact on emails:

  • Notify all after creation / Processor only after update: An email is sent to all recipients when a situation has occurred and to the processor when a situation was updated
  • No notification after closing: No email is sent to anybody
  • Notify all after reopening: An email is sent to all recipients

You can also define custom notification settings and their related impact. Whenever a notification is sent, an email is sent as well.

Email templates are predefined

Situation emails use predefined templates that are automatically assigned to the situation type. This means no additional configuration is required.

The predefined email template consists of the email subject and the email body. Most parts of the email body are fixed and cannot be changed. This contains the greeting, the links to SAP Fiori launchpad and to detailed situation information as well as the source and the sign-off. The customizable email text is the one that describes the situation.

Adapt email subject and email text

You have two options to adapt the email template.

  • Reuse the notification title as email subject and the notification text in the email body.
  • Add a specific plain text to the email body and go with an SAP-defined email subject.

The situation type automatically inherits the setting of the situation template. You can change the settings in the situation type at any time. Afterwards, the new emails are generated according to the updated configuration.

If you change a situation template later, it has no effect on existing situation types.

Reuse notification title and text in email subject and body

You can reuse the notification title as the email subject, including variables such as plant, supplier, or contract ID. The reuse option also inserts the notification text into the email body, where variables can be used as well. The email preview shows how the email will look like.

Additionally, you can select if notifications are aggregated or not.

  • No Aggregation: Send a notification and email for every situation instance.
  • Aggregate by Field: Group notifications as well as emails by a selected field, for instance, supplier.
  • Aggregate All: Group all notifications as well as all emails.

Let’s check how the different settings affect the emails.

Non-aggregated notifications and emails

If you choose non-aggregated notifications, a separate notification and an email are sent for each situation. Initially, notification title and text are already copied from the situation title and text. Title and text can be also reused as the email subject and in the email body. This provides an efficient configuration experience.

The email template contains links to both, the SAP Fiori launchpad and the specific situation.

Reuse option: non-aggregated email

Aggregated notifications and emails

Reuse works the same way for aggregated notifications. Whether you aggregate all notifications or aggregate them by field, the title and text are copied to the email template’s subject and body.

For a meaningful representation, you can use only character-based variables such as strings. In addition, the count variable indicates how many situations are aggregated. If you select Aggregate by Field, you can use the aggregated by variable to show the field used for aggregation.

Because of the aggregation, the email template no longer includes a link to a specific situation; instead, it shows the generic link to SAP Fiori launchpad.

Reuse option: aggregated email by field

Aggregation by field allows more specific end user texts by including this field as variable.

If you aggregate all notifications and emails, the texts will be more generic.

Reuse option: aggregated email for all situations

Note: Be aware of the variables you are using in the email. They may contain personal data. Review the variables to prevent unintended exposure in emails. Also make sure that the data is managed in accordance with the legal requirements and business needs.

Specific email text with SAP-defined email subject

Alternatively, you can add plain texts without variables in the email body and use a default subject defined by SAP. This option makes sure that the email does not include business-sensitive data or personal data which may conflict with legal requirements or business needs.

You also get a preview of the email with this option. Let’s take a look at how this works in detail.

Non-aggregated notifications and emails

For non-aggregated notifications, an email is sent for each situation using the default subject and the embedded custom email text.

The email template includes links to both the SAP Fiori launchpad and the specific situation.

Custom option: non-aggregated email

Aggregated notifications and emails

Aggregated emails also have a default subject and a custom email text without variables.

Because the emails are aggregated, you no longer see the link to a specific situation in the email template but the generic link to SAP Fiori launchpad.

Custom option: aggregated email by field

Since the custom email texts do not contain variables, they remain generic for both aggregation options.

Custom option: aggregated email for all situations

Now you are all set and know how you can configure emails matching your business needs.

Further Information

You find more blog posts in the Best Practices section of Intelligent Situation Handling in the SAP Community. And you can visit SAP Help Portal for further guidance on Situation Handling.

entry.title: Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified - Positioning SAP Business AI Platform
entry.content:
Ready to accelerate your certification journey?

Join the Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified - Positioning SAP Business AI Platform and sharpen the knowledge that matters in just 90 minutes.

We'll cover the certification essentials, including:
• SAP Business AI Platform and its strategic pillars.
• The Autonomous Enterprise vision.
• The value of AI agents.
• How SAP Business Technology Platform connects AI, data, and integration to drive business innovation.

Plus, you'll reinforce key concepts from the SAP Learning Journey and gain practical positioning insights you can use in customer discussions right away.

Whether you're in sales or presales, this is your chance to prepare with purpose and move one step closer to certification.

Register now. Your next certification starts here. https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/jul/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014790.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPBusinessAI #AIInnovation #SAPCertification #PartnerSuccess

entry.title: Problem creating connection between SAC & SAP
entry.content:
Kindly be informed that when we try to create a connection between our SAP and new Test SAC Tenant we face the below error while we are doing the same as what we did with PRD SAC Tenant.

 

and please find the attached screenshot as reference.

 

Please support ASAP.

 

Regards,

Ahmed Essam

entry.title: Re: 创建销项发票界面coding block扩展字段配置
entry.content: Hello, Thank you very much for your post. Can you please let us know your question? Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Missing descriptions when downloading the service cat...
entry.content:
Hi Frank,
Here is short detail for your solution . This usually comes down to the description texts being language dependent, so the entries pull through, but the text come back blank for any language they were not maintained it .
Check the service master texts in the backend and confirm they are actually for your logon language, that's what filled the blanks for us once we added them per language.
Thanks .

entry.title: Re: I can't post a blog on Human Capital Management. What...
entry.content:
"new members who wish to blog will need to take this tutorial to receive blogging permissions"

Source: What You Need to Know About Blogging Post-Migratio... - SAP Community (link obtained from Help - SAP Community)

entry.title: Re: Centralize Authorization Management of SAP Cloud ALM Using the Identity Provisioning Service
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP documentation says:
"If you're working with role mapping to user groups in your identity provider, you need to assign role collections in the SAP BTP cockpit. In this case, you can't view or change roles in the User Management app and use the role request and assignment features in SAP Cloud ALM, so we don't recommend this method."

However, I just tested this setup:

  1. Created a group in IAS (CloudALM_x_calm_GlobalAdministrator).
  2. Provisioned the group and users via IPS to the BTP Cockpit.
  3. The Users from this Groups  automatically received  the  role  x_calm_GlobalAdministrator in BTP 
  4. After about 24 hours, the roles appeared in the Cloud ALM App.

My question:
Even though I use IAS groups and IPS provisioning, I can still edit the roles in the Cloud ALM App.

 

When exactly does the Cloud ALM app block role editing? Why is role editing still possible in my setup?

As far as I understand, this is the only disadvantage of using IPS provisioning.

Thanks for you very much for your help and support.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Error in activating /DMO/FLIGHT pulled from ABAPGIT....
entry.content: Finally I was able to activate it myself. Thanks

entry.title: Company code field to be made mandatory
entry.content:
Currently, the Company Code field in the Guided Sourcing project creation is not mandatory. However, this information is essential for successful integration with our backend systems.

Could you please assist in configuring the system so that the Company Code becomes a mandatory field during project creation?

For your reference, I have attached a screenshot of the current UI showing this field.

entry.title: Cloud ALM SAP Data center's - EU01 ?
entry.content:
Hi team,

I am looking for information regarding the availability of SAP Cloud ALM on the EU01 SAP's hosted datacenter. I have reviewed the official documentation but was unable to find any details on this. Does anyone know if SAP plans to support EU01 region?


Thanks and regards
Karthik

entry.title: Re: Regarding ATP verification based on variant selection
entry.content:
Dear @shiwa,

Yes, with one important qualification. In the standard make-to-order process with Variant Configuration, ATP is normally performed for the top-level configurable product entered in the sales order or for the matched material variant if Variant Matching is used

Configuration-dependent BOM components are not checked individually by sales-order ATP. Components that exist only in the production BOM can be checked later during production planning or production-order processing but this component check does not automatically determine the sales order confirmation date

For standard ATP the main requirements are:

  • 2LN – Basic Available-to-Promise Processing
  • an Availability Check value maintained in the product master
  • the appropriate scope of check configured for the checking group and checking rule
  • sales document and schedule-line settings that trigger the availability check and where required the transfer of requirements

For make-to-order production with Variant Configuration the relevant scope item is 1YT. Scope item 1JW – Advanced Order Promising is only required for additional aATP functions and does not provide a multilevel ATP check of configuration-dependent BOM components

 

More details below:

SAP Help – Order Promising (2LN)

SAP Help – Advanced Order Promising (1JW)

SAP Learning Introducing Basic Available-to-Promise

SAp Leraning Managing Make to Order Process for Product Variants

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (5HP) in SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @Qi_Liu, 

Is there a way for the pricing be maintained by purchasing then adapted to the sales pricing (or vice versa) for this process? Would it be possible that only one side would maintain pricing conditions? 

Thank you very much for your response!

entry.title: Standard asset report- J1IDEPCALPOST and AR02
entry.content:
There are two transaction codes for asset display report J1IDISPBLK and Depreciation Posting J1IDEPCALPOST . In both these reports there is one column of Inv. Supt. under which there are 2 issues:-

1. Negative values are being reflecting in the inv. supt. column. These should be positive values under asset additions column.

2. Secondly same values against asset codes are reflecting in AR02 Report as asset acquisitions.  Sample screenshot of AR02 of sample asset codes enclosed along with asset display report with column inv. Supt. for your reference.

entry.title: Re: License Portal Down - Urgent Renewal for AS ABAP 7.52 Dev Edition (NPL)
entry.content: Hi everyone, I am just following up because my NPL license will expire in just two days (July 17). Since the official portal (https://go.support.sap.com/minisap/#/minisap) is still down, could any SAP moderator or support team member please advise if there is a temporary license key we can use in the meantime? I noticed a few other users in the community are also reporting this portal issue. Any official workaround or advice would be highly appreciated!

entry.title: Include contingent workers in the synch between SuccessFactors and Identity Authentication
entry.content:
Hello,

how do we need to set up the transformation template to include contingent workers in the synch between SF and IAS?

Currently only internal employees are synched but we would like to include contingent workers as to be able to log in to SF.

Kind regards,

Simon

entry.title: Re: Intercompany STO - Use Sales Price (ESP2) as Purchase...
entry.content: Hi Lakshmipathi, for number 4, can I confirm that there should be maintenance of condition records in MM (using Purchase Info Records) and SD (using Manage Prices - Sales)?

entry.title: Re: Contractor/External Manpower Cannot Be Recorded Durin...
entry.content:
Hello @baji_baba_shaik 

Firslty as you have mentioned that a contractor or external employee has done the work then there are two scenarios in which we can take the contractor and external emloyee by using the control key PM02 and PM03.

PM02 --> for contractor
PM03 ---> for external employee

Here in both the cases confirmation is not being done as the confirmation from Material Management(MM) mainly procurement team will do Goods Receipt for PM02 control key as well as service entry sheet for PM03 control key. 

If you need confirmation for these operations as well in IW41 in configuration path you can control this 

SPRO -> SIMG -> plant maintenance and customer service -> maintenance and service processing -> maintenance and service orders -> functions and settings for order types -> control key -> maintain control key 

here you can see the confirmation.

Here you can check the confirmation settings. 

Regards,
Neeraj.

entry.title: Re: MiniSAP Portal down 24+ hours - blocking Developer Access Key for AS ABAP 7.52 trial
entry.content: I guess that @julieplummer20 could help.
